---
title: "Investigate the dynamical stability a GPCR structure"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
---

{{< include /includes/exercise_stab_gpcr_nav.html >}}

## Introduction

This guide will provide a brief overview of a [G protein-coupled receptor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/G_protein-coupled_receptor) (GPCR) system, specifically an adhesion GPCR [@liebscher2022stachel] (or *stachel GPCR*). GPCRs are membrane-embedded proteins, but their structures are generally published without the embedding lipid bilayer, although sometimes lipid molecules bound to specific GPCR binding sites are resolved, like [this cholesterol](https://www.rcsb.org/3d-view/7BVQ/1) in the $\beta$-adrenergic receptor.

Nowadays, there are well-established procedures to embed proteins in lipid bilayer, arguably the most known being [CHARMM-GUI webserver](https://www.charmm-gui.org/). Nevertheless, to better focus on how to set up the simulation box to get the simulations going and analyze data, the starting structure provided in this tutorial is already embedded in a mixed lipid bilayer of cholesterol and phosphatidylcholine (POPC).

<div class="nav-buttons">
  <a href="exercise_gpcr_stability_part_1.qmd" class="nav-btn next-btn">Next &rarr;</a>
</div>

## References
